Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order To Compel Defendant To Attend And Testify At Deposition And Produce For Inspection Documents Describes In Deposition Notice And For Order Imposing Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant
Off Calendar Jun-22-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Roger L. Meredith, Esq., for Plaintiff and moving party: Marcella Faulk
Anthony Bass, defendant and opposing party in Propria Persona

After hearing, tentative ruling is adopted as modified. Plaintiff Marcella Faulk's Motion to compel defendant to attend deposition and testify at deposition and produce documents described in deposition notice and for order imposing sanctions against defendant granted as follows:
1. Within ten days of service of this order, defendant Bass shall appear for, and testify at deposition on a date, time and place to be specified by plaintiff or her counsel; but no later thatn July 24, 2009.
2. At the time of his appearance at deposition, defendant shall produce any documents described in categories 1 through 7, and 9 through 12, inclusive, of the deposition notice, which were not produced in response to previous request for production of documents, or which have been discovered, acquired, or become available since the time of that previous response;
3. In addition, at the time of his appearance at deposition, defendant shall produce any documents described in category 8 of the deposition notice;
4. Request for sanctions granted; sanctions shall issue in the sum of $1,090.50, which includes court reporter appearance fee of $300.50, filing fee of $40.00, and attorneys' fees in the sum of $750.00. Imposition of sanctions is stayed for 90 days from date of notice of entry of order. Commissioner: Paul Slavit. Not reported.

Notice Of Motion For Enforcement Of Settlement Or For An Order Setting Matter For Trial
Off Calendar Mar-09-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF MARCELLA FAULK'S Motion for Enforcement of Settlement or for an Order Setting Matter for Trial is denied. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Court cannot enter judgment in favor of plaintiff because there is insufficient showing of defendant's direct participation in the stipulation for entry of judgment. See Levy v. Superior Court (1995) 10 Cal 4th 578. Plaintiff to contact Dept. 212 to be placed back on the trial calendar. (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR 5759
